description: App Registration Has an Overly Broad Federated Identity Credential
rationale: A federated identity credential (Workload Identity Federation) lets an external OIDC identity - such as a GitHub Actions workflow, GitLab pipeline, or Azure DevOps job - authenticate as the application with no client secret or certificate, inheriting all of the application's permissions. When the trusted subject is overly broad (a wildcard, any pull request, or a subject not pinned to a specific branch, tag, or environment), anyone who can execute in that external context can impersonate the application. For example, a credential trusting <samp>repo:org/repo:pull_request</samp> can be exercised by any pull request - including from a fork - and a wildcard subject trusts many workflows at once. The impact scales with the permissions granted to the application.
remediation: Review each flagged federated identity credential and tighten its <samp>subject</samp> to the narrowest identity that needs it: pin to a specific branch (<samp>repo:org/repo:ref:refs/heads/main</samp>), a protected environment (<samp>repo:org/repo:environment:Production</samp>), or a specific reusable workflow, and avoid wildcards and <samp>pull_request</samp> subjects. Remove unused credentials. Ensure applications reachable via federation follow least privilege for their API permissions and Azure roles.
